calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Verified Petition Of Michael R. Kembel For Appointment As Successor Trustee; For Removal Of Proposed Successor Trustes Or Objections To Their Appointment
Off Calendar May-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Dwight Moore and Kelly Dearman (Petitioners). David Olick for Michael Kembell.

The Court informed Counsel that it had selected a Successor Trustee and that it had prepared an order appointing the Successor Trustee. In addition, the Court stated that the proposed order had been prepared with some suggestion of the Successor Trustee due to the unique title situation with the proposed property, that the Successor Trustee is to have title of the property, is to transfer title of the property to the proposed purchaser and distribute the proceeds pursuant to the terms of the settlement agreement that was placed on the record before Judge Goldsmith. Both Counsel reviewed the proposed order in open court. The order was also signed in open Court. The Clerk will file the order and mail copies to Counsel.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

JUDICIAL MEDIATION
Off Calendar May-17-2010 Text Ruling

Verified Petition Of Michael R. Kembel For Appointment As Successor Trustee; For Removal Of Proposed Successor Trustees Or Objections To Their Appointment
Off Calendar May-04-2010 Text Ruling

Petitioner'S Petition And Request For Appointment Of A Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for Ina Dearman. John Boessenecker for Kelly Dearman and. Richard Canatella and David Olick for Michael Kembel. Susan Rushakoff specially appearing for the San Francisco Aids Foundation.

The Court stated that there was no opposition to the Appointment of a Successor Trustee, that the proposed Successor Trustee had questions and that an e-mail was sent to Counsel about the questions, that it understands that there is new Counsel in the case, and that the parties want to mediate. Mr. Olick stated that Michael has collected rent, that the Successor Trustee could be compensated by a noticed motion, that the taxes have been paid for by Michael, that the Moore's have not paid rent in seven years, that there is a proposal out there to possibly sell the property, that the Moore's would be evicted and then the parties could mediate the division, and that the principal problem is a wasting asset. Mr. Boessenecker stated that the only thing that hasn't been paid is the taxes, that Michael Kembel collects rents from the third unit, that one unit is occupied by Mr. Kembel another by his clients, that the third unit is rented, and that there was no agreement by his clients to pay rent. In addition, Mr Boessenecker stated that his clients have invested $250,000.00 in the property, that they have a half-vested interest, that they are not tenants, that they won't agree to a sale until title is settled, and that they want the Successor Trustee appointed today. Mr. Olick presented additional argument. Mr. Koplowitz suggested setting up mediation immediately. The Court ordered the matter to mediaton (ADR) immediately, stated that it would appoint a Successor Trustee, and set status on the mediation for 5/24/10 at 11:00 a.m. in D-204. The Court stated that it would prepare a minute order naming the Successor Trustee with the instructions. Mr. Boessenecker will then prep the order
Judge: Paul Alvarado. Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda.

Petitioner'S Petition And Request For Appointment Of A Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Continued To May-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: Appearances for Tuesday, MAR-23-2010, 1:30 p.m., Dept. 503: JOHN E. BOESSENECKER, Esq. for Kelly Dearman and Dwight Moore, Petitioners, Kembel Trust; ERNEST F. DER, Esq. for S.F. AIDS Foundation, Beneficiary of Trust; RICHARD A. CNATELLA, Esq. and DAVID ORICK, Esq. for Mr. Kembel. 1:40 p.m.: The Court takes the bench; on the record Court and counsel convene and discuss Petitioner's Petition for Appointment of a Successor Trustee, on calendar this day. 1:54 p.m.: The Court orders a recess for all counsel to convene to discuss the matter. 3:16 p.m.: All counsel re-convene on the record. The Court is informed that all counsel agree to have the Court choose and appoint a Successor Trustee. The Court orders the matter continued until Tuesday, May-04-2010, 9:00AM in Dept. 204, for Status of Appointment of a Successor Trustee on that date and time. All counsel stipulate and are to appear. The Court advises counsel that a trial date to be selected and the Court and Dept. 206 personnel to be notified of several possible dates when counsel are available for trail.

JUDGE: HON. PAUL H. ALVARADO; CLERK MARJORIE L. MANZELLA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600;
(DEPARTMENT #503).

Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee.
Off Calendar Mar-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for San Francisco Aids Foundation. John Boessenecker for and with Kelly Dearman and for Dwight Moore. Richard Canatella and David Olick for Michael Kembell.

The Court informed all Parties that the matter was singly assigned to Judge Alvarado in 2008 for all purposes and that it remains assigned to him. The Clerk contacted Dept. 206 and advised the Clerk to set the matter (Petition for Order Appointing Successor Trustee) for March 23, 2010 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept. 503. The Court advised Counsel to contact the Department of the Presiding Judge (206) to schedule matters.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jul-10-2008 Continued To Oct-20-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Application For Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-01-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for and with Kelly Dearman and Dwight Moore (Petitioners).

This matter involved a dispute over access to a building where the petitioner's are tenants. Mr. Boessenecker stated that there was an agreement between Fitz Fleming and the parties about 8 years ago to move into the building, sell there home in Pacifica and to use the proceeds of $300,000.00 to repair and renovate the property for exchange of one half ownership. Mr. Fitz passed away without completing his end of the bargain. The Court issued the temporary restraining order and set the Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction to be heard on July 14, 2008 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Raymond Arata. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto CSR#5229. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
